records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4691 - It is narrated:
"Whoever dies without leaving behind a successor, it is as if he was never among the people. And whoever dies and leaves behind a successor, it is as if he never died."
records.arabicText: 4691 - وَ رُوِيَ: "أَنَّ مَنْ مَاتَ بِلاَ خَلَفٍ فَكَأَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ فِي اَلنَّاسِ وَ مَنْ مَاتَ وَ لَهُ خَلَفٌ فَكَأَنْ لَمْ يَمُتْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4691 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever dies without leaving behind a successor, it is as if he was never among the people. And whoever dies and leaves behind a successor, it is as if he never died."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4692 - Aban ibn Taghlib narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: "Daughters are good deeds, and sons are blessings. Good deeds are rewarded, and blessings are questioned about."
records.arabicText: 4692 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانُ بْنُ تَغْلِبَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "اَلْبَنَاتُ حَسَنَاتٌ وَ اَلْبَنُونَ نِعْمَةٌ فَالْحَسَنَاتُ يُثَابُ عَلَيْهَا وَ اَلنِّعْمَةُ يُسْأَلُ عَنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4692 - Aban ibn Taghlib narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Daughters are good deeds, and sons are blessings. Good deeds are rewarded, and blessings are questioned about."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4693 - The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) was given the glad tidings of a daughter. He (sw) looked at the faces of his companions and saw displeasure in them.
He (sw) said: "What is the matter with you? She is a sweet flower (rayḥanah) that I smell, and her sustenance is upon Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."
And Prophet (as) was a father of daughters.
records.arabicText: 4693 - : وَ بُشِّرَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِابْنَةٍ فَنَظَرَ فِي وُجُوهِ أَصْحَابِهِ فَرَأَى اَلْكَرَاهَةَ فِيهِمْ فَقَالَ "مَا لَكُمْ رَيْحَانَةٌ أَشَمُّهَا وَ رِزْقُهَا عَلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ" وَ كَانَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَبَا بَنَاتٍ.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4694 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "When a child falls ill, it serves as an expiation for the sins of the parents."
records.arabicText: 4694 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "فِي اَلْمَرَضِ يُصِيبُ اَلصَّبِيَّ إِنَّهُ كَفَّارَةٌ لِوَالِدَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4694 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a child falls ill, it serves as an expiation for the sins of the parents."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4695 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has mercy on a man because of the intensity of his love for his child."
records.arabicText: 4695 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَيَرْحَمُ اَلرَّجُلَ لِشِدَّةِ حُبِّهِ لِوَلَدِهِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4696 - Umar ibn Yazid said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as): "I have daughters."
The Imam (as) replied: "Perhaps you wish for their death? Know that if you wish for their death and they die, you will not be rewarded on the Day of Judgment, and you will meet your Lord (azj) while being disobedient to Him (swt)."
records.arabicText: 4696 - وَ - قَالَ لَهُ عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ : إِنَّ لِي بَنَاتٍ فَقَالَ "لَعَلَّكَ تَتَمَنَّى مَوْتَهُنَّ أَمَا إِنَّكَ إِنْ تَمَنَّيْتَ مَوْتَهُنَّ وَ مِتْنَ لَمْ تُؤْجَرْ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ وَ لَقِيتَ رَبَّكَ حِينَ تَلْقَاهُ وَ أَنْتَ عَاصٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4696 - Umar ibn Yazid said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I have daughters."
The Imam (as) replied: "Perhaps you wish for their death? Know that if you wish for their death and they die, you will not be rewarded on the Day of Judgment, and you will meet your Lord (azj) while being disobedient to Him (swt)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4697 - Hamzah ibn Humran narrated through his chain of transmission that a man came to the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) while another man was sitting with Him (sw).
The man informed the Prophet (sw) about the birth of his child, and the color of the man's face changed.
The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) asked him: "What is the matter with you?"
The man replied: "It's nothing."
The Prophet (sw) said: "Speak."
The man said: "I left while my wife was in labor, and I was informed that she gave birth to a daughter."
The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said to him: "The earth will carry her, the sky will shade her, and Allah (swt) will provide for her. She is a sweet flower that you will smell."
Then the Prophet (sw) turned to his companions and said: "Whoever has one daughter will be burdened with care.
Whoever has two daughters, then seek help from Allah (swt) for him.
Whoever has three daughters, the obligation of jihad and every hardship is lifted from him.
And whoever has four daughters, O servants of Allah (swt), help him! O servants of Allah (swt), support him! O servants of Allah (swt), have mercy on him!"
records.arabicText: 4697 - وَ رَوَى حَمْزَةُ بْنُ حُمْرَانَ بِإِسْنَادِهِ: أَنَّهُ أَتَى رَجُلٌ إِلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ عِنْدَهُ رَجُلٌ فَأَخْبَرَهُ بِمَوْلُودٍ لَهُ فَتَغَيَّرَ لَوْنُ اَلرَّجُلِ فَقَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "مَا لَكَ" قَالَ خَيْرٌ قَالَ "قُلْ" قَالَ خَرَجْتُ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ تَمْخَضُ فَأُخْبِرْتُ أَنَّهَا وَلَدَتْ جَارِيَةً فَقَالَ لَهُ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "اَلْأَرْضُ تُقِلُّهَا وَ اَلسَّمَاءُ تُظِلُّهَا وَ اَللَّهُ يَرْزُقُهَا وَ هِيَ رَيْحَانَةٌ تَشَمُّهَا" ثُمَّ أَقْبَلَ عَلَى أَصْحَابِهِ فَقَالَ "مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ اِبْنَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ فَهُوَ مَقْرُوحٌ وَ مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ اِبْنَتَانِ فَيَا غَوْثَاهْ بِاللَّهِ وَ مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ ثَلاَثُ بَنَاتٍ وُضِعَ عَنْهُ اَلْجِهَادُ وَ كُلُّ مَكْرُوهٍ وَ مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ أَرْبَعُ بَنَاتٍ فَيَا عِبَادَ اَللَّهِ أَعِينُوهُ يَا عِبَادَ اَللَّهِ أَقْرِضُوهُ يَا عِبَادَ اَللَّهِ اِرْحَمُوهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4697 - Hamzah ibn Humran narrated through his chain of transmission that
records.thaqalaynMatn:
a man came to the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) while another man was sitting with Him (sw).
The man informed the Prophet (sw) about the birth of his child, and the color of the man's face changed.
The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) asked him: "What is the matter with you?"
The man replied: "It's nothing."
The Prophet (sw) said: "Speak."
The man said: "I left while my wife was in labor, and I was informed that she gave birth to a daughter."
The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said to him: "The earth will carry her, the sky will shade her, and Allah (swt) will provide for her. She is a sweet flower that you will smell."
Then the Prophet (sw) turned to his companions and said: "Whoever has one daughter will be burdened with care.
Whoever has two daughters, then seek help from Allah (swt) for him.
Whoever has three daughters, the obligation of jihad and every hardship is lifted from him.
And whoever has four daughters, O servants of Allah (swt), help him! O servants of Allah (swt), support him! O servants of Allah (swt), have mercy on him!"

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4698 - The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
"Whoever takes care of three daughters or three sisters, Paradise becomes obligatory for him." It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), what about two?"
He (sw) replied: "And two (meaning same reward)."
It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), and one?"
He (sw) said: "And one (meaning same reward)."
records.arabicText: 4698 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: "مَنْ عَالَ ثَلاَثَ بَنَاتٍ أَوْ ثَلاَثَ أَخَوَاتٍ وَجَبَتْ لَهُ اَلْجَنَّةُ " قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ وَ اِثْنَتَيْنِ قَالَ "وَ اِثْنَتَيْنِ" قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ "وَ وَاحِدَةً" قَالَ "وَ وَاحِدَةً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4698 - The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever takes care of three daughters or three sisters, Paradise becomes obligatory for him." It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), what about two?"
He (sw) replied: "And two (meaning same reward)."
It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), and one?"
He (sw) said: "And one (meaning same reward)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4699 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever takes care of two daughters, two sisters, two paternal aunts, or two maternal aunts, they will shield him from the Fire."
records.arabicText:
4699 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
"مَنْ عَالَ اِبْنَتَيْنِ أَوْ أُخْتَيْنِ أَوْ عَمَّتَيْنِ أَوْ خَالَتَيْنِ حَجَبَتَاهُ مِنَ اَلنَّارِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4699 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever takes care of two daughters, two sisters, two paternal aunts, or two maternal aunts, they will shield him from the Fire."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4700 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When a man is blessed with a daughter, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, sends an angel to her who passes his wing over her head and chest and says, 'A fragile being created from weakness; whoever provides for her will be assisted.'"
records.arabicText:
4700 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
"إِذَا أَصَابَ اَلرَّجُلُ اِبْنَةً بَعَثَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَيْهَا مَلَكاً فَأَمَرَّ جَنَاحَهُ عَلَى رَأْسِهَا وَ صَدْرِهَا وَ قَالَ ضَعِيفَةٌ خُلِقَتْ مِنْ ضَعْفٍ اَلْمُنْفِقُ عَلَيْهَا مُعَانٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4700 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a man is blessed with a daughter, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, sends an angel to her who passes his wing over her head and chest and says, 'A fragile being created from weakness; whoever provides for her will be assisted.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4701 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "Know that one of you will find his miscarried child waiting anxiously at the gate of Paradise. When they (the child) sees him, they will grasp his hand and not let go until he brings him into Paradise. Indeed, if one of your children dies, you will be rewarded for it, and if the child remains after you, he will seek forgiveness for you after your death."
records.arabicText:
4701 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ :
"اِعْلَمُوا أَنَّ أَحَدَكُمْ يَلْقَى سِقْطَهُ مُحْبَنْطِئاً عَلَى بَابِ اَلْجَنَّةِ حَتَّى إِذَا رَآهُ أَخَذَ بِيَدِهِ حَتَّى يُدْخِلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةَ وَ إِنَّ وَلَدَ أَحَدِكُمْ إِذَا مَاتَ أُجِرَ فِيهِ وَ إِنْ بَقِيَ بَعْدَهُ اِسْتَغْفَرَ لَهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4701 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Know that one of you will find his miscarried child waiting anxiously at the gate of Paradise. When they (the child) sees him, they will grasp his hand and not let go until he brings him into Paradise. Indeed, if one of your children dies, you will be rewarded for it, and if the child remains after you, he will seek forgiveness for you after your death."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4702 - The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "Love the children and have mercy on them. When you promise them something, fulfill it for them, for they only perceive that you are the ones who provide for them."
records.arabicText: 4702 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: "أَحِبُّوا اَلصِّبْيَانَ وَ اِرْحَمُوهُمْ وَ إِذَا وَعَدْتُمُوهُمْ فَفُوا لَهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ لاَ يَرَوْنَ إِلاَّ أَنَّكُمْ تَرْزُقُونَهُمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4702 - The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Love the children and have mercy on them. When you promise them something, fulfill it for them, for they only perceive that you are the ones who provide for them."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4703 - Rifa'ah ibn Musa narrated from Abu Al-Hasan (as) who said:
I asked him about a man who has sons from different mothers, can he favor one of them over the others?
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, there is no harm in that. My father (as) used to favor me over Abdullah."
records.arabicText: 4703 - وَ رَوَى رِفَاعَةُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ بَنُونَ وَ أُمُّهُمْ لَيْسَتْ بِوَاحِدَةٍ أَ يُفَضِّلُ أَحَدَهُمْ عَلَى اَلْآخَرِ قَالَ "نَعَمْ لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ قَدْ كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُفَضِّلُنِي عَلَى عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4703 - Rifa'ah ibn Musa narrated from Abu Al-Hasan (as) who said:
I asked him about a man who has sons from different mothers, can he favor one of them over the others?
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, there is no harm in that. My father (as) used to favor me over Abdullah."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4704 - In the narration of Al-Sakooni, it is reported that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) saw a man who had two sons.
The man kissed one of them and left the other.
The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said to him, "Why did you not treat them equally?"
records.arabicText: 4704 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ قَالَ: نَظَرَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِلَى رَجُلٍ لَهُ اِبْنَانِ فَقَبَّلَ أَحَدَهُمَا وَ تَرَكَ اَلْآخَرَ فَقَالَ لَهُ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "فَهَلاَّ وَاسَيْتَ بَيْنَهُمَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4704 - In the narration of Al-Sakooni, it is reported that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) saw a man who had two sons.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The man kissed one of them and left the other.
The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said to him, "Why did you not treat them equally?"

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4705 - Imam (as) said: "Parents are held accountable for disobedience toward their children just as children are held accountable for disobedience toward their parents."
records.arabicText: 4705 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: "يَلْزَمُ اَلْوَالِدَيْنِ مِنْ عُقُوقِ اَلْوَلَدِ مَا يَلْزَمُ اَلْوَلَدَ لَهُمَا مِنَ اَلْعُقُوقِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4705 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Parents are held accountable for disobedience toward their children just as children are held accountable for disobedience toward their parents."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4706 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"A man's kindness to his child is a form of his kindness to his own parents."
records.arabicText: 4706 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "بِرُّ اَلرَّجُلِ بِوَلَدِهِ بِرُّهُ بِوَالِدَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4706 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A man's kindness to his child is a form of his kindness to his own parents."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4707 - In another narration, the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "Whoever has a child should act childishly with him."
records.arabicText: 4707 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ قَالَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ كَانَ عِنْدَهُ صَبِيٌّ فَلْيَتَصَابَ لَهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4707 - In another narration, the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever has a child should act childishly with him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4708 - Imam (as) said:
"Among the blessings of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, upon a man is that his child resembles him."
records.arabicText: 4708 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: "مِنْ نِعَمِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَى اَلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُشْبِهَهُ وَلَدُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4708 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Among the blessings of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, upon a man is that his child resembles him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4709 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, wills to create a being,
He (swt) gathers every form between him and Adam (as), then creates him in the image of one of them. Therefore, no one should say about his child, 'This one does not resemble me or any of my ancestors.'"
records.arabicText: 4709 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَخْلُقَ خَلْقاً جَمَعَ كُلَّ صُورَةٍ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ آدَمَ ثُمَّ خَلَقَهُ عَلَى صُورَةِ إِحْدَاهُنَّ فَلاَ يَقُولَنَّ أَحَدٌ لِوَلَدِهِ هَذَا لاَ يُشْبِهُنِي وَ لاَ يُشْبِهُ شَيْئاً مِنْ آبَائِي".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4709 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, wills to create a being,
He (swt) gathers every form between him and Adam (as), then creates him in the image of one of them. Therefore, no one should say about his child, 'This one does not resemble me or any of my ancestors.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Aqiqah, Tahnik, Naming, Kunyah, Shaving the newborn's Head, Piercing the Ears, and Circumcision
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4710 - Umar ibn Yazid narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: "Every person is held in pledge on the Day of Judgment by their Aqiqah, and the Aqiqah is more obligatory than the sacrificial offering (Udhiyyah)."
records.arabicText: 4710 - رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: "كُلُّ اِمْرِئٍ مُرْتَهَنٌ، يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ بِعَقِيقَتِهِ وَ اَلْعَقِيقَةُ أَوْجَبُ مِنَ اَلْأُضْحِيَّةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4710 - Umar ibn Yazid narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Every person is held in pledge on the Day of Judgment by their Aqiqah, and the Aqiqah is more obligatory than the sacrificial offering (Udhiyyah)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Aqiqah, Tahnik, Naming, Kunyah, Shaving the newborn's Head, Piercing the Ears, and Circumcision
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4711 - In the narration of Abu Khadijah from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
"Every person is held in pledge by their natural disposition (fitrah), and every newborn is held in pledge by their Aqiqah."
records.arabicText: 3711 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ أَبِي خَدِيجَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "كُلُّ إِنْسَانٍ مُرْتَهَنٌ بِالْفِطْرَةِ وَ كُلُّ مَوْلُودٍ مُرْتَهَنٌ بِالْعَقِيقَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4711 - In the narration of Abu Khadijah from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Every person is held in pledge by their natural disposition (fitrah), and every newborn is held in pledge by their Aqiqah."

records.chapter: Chapter on Aqiqah, Tahnik, Naming, Kunyah, Shaving the newborn's Head, Piercing the Ears, and Circumcision
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4712 - It was narrated from Umar ibn Yazid who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "By Allah (swt), I do not know whether my father performed the aqiqah (sacrifice for a newborn) for me or not."
Imam (as) instructed me, so I performed the aqiqah for myself while I was an old man.
records.arabicText: 4712 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ اَللَّهِ مَا أَدْرِي أَ كَانَ أَبِي عَقَّ عَنِّي أَمْ لاَ فَأَمَرَنِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَعَقَقْتُ عَنْ نَفْسِي وَ أَنَا شَيْخٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4712 - It was narrated from Umar ibn Yazid who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "By Allah (swt), I do not know whether my father performed the aqiqah (sacrifice for a newborn) for me or not."
Imam (as) instructed me, so I performed the aqiqah for myself while I was an old man.

records.chapter: Chapter on Aqiqah, Tahnik, Naming, Kunyah, Shaving the newborn's Head, Piercing the Ears, and Circumcision
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4713 - In the narration of Ali ibn Al-Hakam from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from the Righteous Servant (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)), that he said:
"Aqiqah is obligatory when a man is blessed with a child. If he wishes to name the child on the same day, he may do so."
records.arabicText: 4713 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنِ اَلْعَبْدِ اَلصَّالِحِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "اَلْعَقِيقَةُ وَاجِبَةٌ إِذَا وُلِدَ لِلرَّجُلِ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ يُسَمِّيَهُ مِنْ يَوْمِهِ فَعَلَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4713 - In the narration of Ali ibn Al-Hakam from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from the Righteous Servant (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Aqiqah is obligatory when a man is blessed with a child. If he wishes to name the child on the same day, he may do so."

records.chapter: Chapter on Aqiqah, Tahnik, Naming, Kunyah, Shaving the newborn's Head, Piercing the Ears, and Circumcision
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4714 - Ammar Al-Sabati narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
"Aqiqah is obligatory for those who are wealthy. As for the poor, when they become financially capable, they should perform it. If they are unable to do so, then nothing is required of them.
If Aqiqah was not done for a person until a sacrifice (Udhiyyah) was offered on their behalf, the sacrifice suffices for them. Every newborn is held in pledge by their Aqiqah."
Imam (as) also said regarding Aqiqah: "A ram should be slaughtered on their behalf. If a ram is not available, anything permissible for sacrifice in Udhiyyah will suffice. If that is not possible, then a lamb, the largest among the lambs of the year, should be offered."
records.arabicText: 4714 - وَ رَوَى عَمَّارٌ اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "اَلْعَقِيقَةُ لاَزِمَةٌ لِمَنْ كَانَ غَنِيّاً وَ مَنْ كَانَ فَقِيراً إِذَا أَيْسَرَ فَعَلَ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَقْدِرْ عَلَى ذَلِكَ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَعُقَّ عَنْهُ حَتَّى ضَحَّى عَنْهُ فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَتْهُ اَلْأُضْحِيَّةُ وَ كُلُّ مَوْلُودٍ مُرْتَهَنٌ بِعَقِيقَتِهِ" وَ قَالَ فِي اَلْعَقِيقَةِ "يُذْبَحُ عَنْهُ كَبْشٌ فَإِنْ لَمْ يُوجَدْ كَبْشٌ أَجْزَأَهُ مَا يُجْزِي فِي اَلْأُضْحِيَّةِ وَ إِلاَّ فَحَمَلٌ أَعْظَمُ مَا يَكُونُ مِنْ حُمْلاَنِ اَلسَّنَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4714 - Ammar Al-Sabati narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Aqiqah is obligatory for those who are wealthy. As for the poor, when they become financially capable, they should perform it. If they are unable to do so, then nothing is required of them.
If Aqiqah was not done for a person until a sacrifice (Udhiyyah) was offered on their behalf, the sacrifice suffices for them. Every newborn is held in pledge by their Aqiqah."
Imam (as) also said regarding Aqiqah: "A ram should be slaughtered on their behalf. If a ram is not available, anything permissible for sacrifice in Udhiyyah will suffice. If that is not possible, then a lamb, the largest among the lambs of the year, should be offered."

records.chapter: Chapter on Aqiqah, Tahnik, Naming, Kunyah, Shaving the newborn's Head, Piercing the Ears, and Circumcision
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4715 - In the narration of Muhammad ibn Marid from Abu Abdullah (as), he said: I asked him about the Aqiqah.
Imam (as) replied: "It can be a sheep, a cow, or a camel. Then the child should be named, and his head should be shaved on the seventh day. Charity should be given in gold or silver equal to the weight of the shaved hair. If the newborn is a boy, a male animal should be sacrificed on his behalf, and if it is a girl, a female animal should be sacrificed for her."
records.arabicText: 4715 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَارِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْعَقِيقَةِ فَقَالَ "شَاةٌ أَوْ بَقَرَةٌ أَوْ بَدَنَةٌ ثُمَّ يُسَمِّي وَ يَحْلِقُ رَأْسَ اَلْمَوْلُودِ يَوْمَ اَلسَّابِعِ وَ يَتَصَدَّقُ بِوَزْنِ شَعْرِهِ ذَهَباً أَوْ فِضَّةً فَإِنْ كَانَ ذَكَراً عَقَّ عَنْهُ ذَكَراً وَ إِنْ كَانَ أُنْثَى عَقَّ عَنْهَا أُنْثَى".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4715 - In the narration of Muhammad ibn Marid from Abu Abdullah (as), he said: I asked him about the Aqiqah.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It can be a sheep, a cow, or a camel. Then the child should be named, and his head should be shaved on the seventh day. Charity should be given in gold or silver equal to the weight of the shaved hair. If the newborn is a boy, a male animal should be sacrificed on his behalf, and if it is a girl, a female animal should be sacrificed for her."
